title: ferray-linalg: pinv ignores rcond parameter
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

pinv() in solve.rs accepts _rcond: Option<T> but ignores it — the parameter is prefixed with underscore and never used. It delegates to faer's pseudoinverse() which presumably uses its own default tolerance. NumPy's pinv uses rcond to determine which singular values to treat as zero (singular values below rcond * max_sv are zeroed in the inverse). This affects numerical results for ill-conditioned matrices.
